model: deepseek-v4-pro sharded review with deepseek-v4-flash synthesis

non_blocking_findings.summary: DeepSeek noted the no-code mutation guard is invoked only in the provider/model-routed path; this preserves current behavior because local-bounded completion is governed separately and the existing runner test covers provider mutation fail-closed.
DeepSeek noted the primary worktree fallback path is environment-specific; this was moved without semantic change and remains overrideable through primary_worktree_path/primaryWorktreePath.
DeepSeek noted staged dispatch does not run execution-scope isolation; this is intentional because staged dispatch only marks provider work as running and does not execute or complete work packages.

Initial plan wording said tests pass/fail as before; the plan was updated to name the preserved contracts explicitly: fixed owned_files gate ordering, isolated worktree requirement, and fail-closed no-code mutation guard.

scope.diff_summary: Context work package execution-scope and workspace mutation guard logic was extracted from src/workflow/context-work-package-runner.js into a 156-line src/workflow/context-work-package-execution-scope.js module. The runner decreased from 1217 to 1135 lines while preserving fixed-development owned_files gate ordering, primary-worktree blocking for code-output packages, and fail-closed no-code provider workspace mutation guard behavior.

notes: DeepSeek source, test, fixed-mode/manifest, and plan shards all returned PASS and synthesis returned PASS. The review confirmed owned_files fixed-mode gate ordering still precedes provider adapter execution, code-output work packages still cannot complete from the primary platform worktree, no-code provider mutation remains fail-closed, and plan metadata now closes P5 only while leaving remaining large-file queue items tracked separately.
